text: {"candidates": [{"index": 0, "question": "First, find the circumference of a circle with a radius of 26. Then, using that value as the new radius, calculate the volume of a cylinder with a height of 19. Finally, determine the square root of that volume."}, {"index": 1, "question": "Find the aspect ratio of a rectangle that is 64 by 28. Then, compute the ratio of that aspect ratio to 21. Using that new ratio, check if it is within the range from 28 to 222."}, {"index": 2, "question": "Calculate which day of the year occurs 54 days after day 122. Then, increase that day number by 25 percent. Finally, find the square root of the increased value."}, {"index": 3, "question": "Convert 400 milliliters to fluid ounces. Then, using that result, calculate the percentage change from it to 651. Finally, compute the percentage change from that new result to 429."}, {"index": 4, "question": "Calculate the utilization percentage when 419 out of 1062 slots are in use. Then, multiply that percentage by 22. Finally, round the resulting product to zero decimal places."}]}

candidates.question: First, find the circumference of a circle with a radius of 26. Then, using that value as the new radius, calculate the volume of a cylinder with a height of 19. Finally, determine the square root of that volume.
Find the aspect ratio of a rectangle that is 64 by 28. Then, compute the ratio of that aspect ratio to 21. Using that new ratio, check if it is within the range from 28 to 222.
Calculate which day of the year occurs 54 days after day 122. Then, increase that day number by 25 percent. Finally, find the square root of the increased value.
Convert 400 milliliters to fluid ounces. Then, using that result, calculate the percentage change from it to 651. Finally, compute the percentage change from that new result to 429.
Calculate the utilization percentage when 419 out of 1062 slots are in use. Then, multiply that percentage by 22. Finally, round the resulting product to zero decimal places.
